etymology.description: semantic 广 (wide, extensive) + phonetic 尚 (shàng) -> open
etymology.interpretation: The character 敞(chǎng, 'open') combines the semantic element 广(廣) (guǎng, 'wide, extensive'), which conveys the idea of an expansive area, with the phonetic component 尚(尚) (shàng), which contributes the sound. The semantic component suggests opening or widening a space, leading to the meaning of 'open'.

examples.chinese: 房门敞开着(房門敞開著)。
他喜欢在敞亮的空间工作(他喜歡在敞亮的空間工作)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài chǎngliàng de kōngjiān gōngzuò.
examples.english: He likes working in bright, open spaces.

examples.chinese: 天气晴朗时(天氣晴朗時)，我们在沙滩上敞开心扉(我們在沙灘上敞開心扉)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānqì qínglǎng shí, wǒmen zài shātān shàng chǎngkāi xīnfēi.
examples.english: When the weather is clear, we open our hearts on the beach.
